refnamediv.refpurpose: daemon to set UID and GID mappings for user namespaces

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: newidmapd is a systemd1 socket-activated service that performs the privileged write of UID and GID mappings into a process's user namespace on behalf of unprivileged callers.

Writing to /proc/pid/uid_map and /proc/pid/gid_map normally requires elevated privileges. newidmapd accepts requests over a Varlink socket, validates them, and writes the mappings itself, so that tools such as newuidmap1 and newgidmap1 need no setuid bit.
Before writing any mapping, newidmapd verifies that:

listitem.para: The calling process owns the target process (the /proc/pid directory is owned by the caller's UID and GID).
Every requested subordinate ID range is listed for the caller in /etc/subuid (for UID mappings) or /etc/subgid (for GID mappings). A process is always allowed to map its own UID or GID.
The number of mapping entries does not exceed the kernel limit of 340 ranges (enforced since Linux 4.16).

refsect1.para: When launched by systemd1 via socket activation, the daemon exits automatically after 30 seconds of inactivity.

listitem.para: Enable socket activation mode. In this mode the daemon inherits the listening socket from systemd and exits automatically after 30 seconds of idle time. This is the standard mode when running under systemd.
Enable debug mode (maximum log verbosity).
Enable verbose logging.
Give the help list.
Print program version.

refsect1.title: Varlink Interface
refsect1.para: newidmapd exposes the interface org.openSUSE.newidmapd over the Varlink socket /run/account/newidmapd-socket. The interface provides the following methods:

listitem.para: Writes UID or GID mappings for the user namespace of a given process. The caller must own the target process. The requested ranges are validated against /etc/subuid or /etc/subgid as appropriate.
Input fields:
Process ID of the target process whose namespace mapping is to be written.
Which mapping file to write. Must be either uid_map or gid_map.

Array of mapping entries. Each entry is a MapRange object with three integer fields: upper (first ID inside the namespace), lower (first ID outside the namespace), and count (length of the range). At most 340 entries are accepted.
Output fields:
Whether the operation succeeded.
Human-readable error description on failure.
Returns Alive=true if the service is running. Useful to check that the daemon is reachable before issuing other calls.
Requests the daemon to stop. An optional integer ExitCode can be supplied; it will be used as the process exit status.
Adjusts the maximum log level at runtime. The Level field uses BSD syslog integer values (0 = emergency … 7 = debug).
Returns the daemon's current environment block as an array of strings in KEY=VALUE form.

refsect1.para: The interface defines the following error types:

listitem.para: The caller does not own the target process, or the requested mapping ranges are not permitted by the subordinate ID configuration.
A required parameter was missing, out of range, or otherwise invalid.
An unexpected internal error occurred while processing the request.
The daemon binary.
Varlink socket used to communicate with the daemon. Created with mode 0666 so any local user can connect.
Defines the subordinate UID ranges allocated to each user. Consulted when validating uid_map requests.
Defines the subordinate GID ranges allocated to each user. Consulted when validating gid_map requests.
Kernel file written to apply UID mappings to a user namespace.
Kernel file written to apply GID mappings to a user namespace.

refsect1.title: See Also
refsect1.para: newuidmap1, newgidmap1, subuid5, subgid5, user_namespaces7, systemd1
